original_prompt: Please create a new 1-hour long event 'Follow-up meeting' on 2024-05-16 at 10:00 with the same participants as the 'Introductory meeting' event on 2024-05-15. The description should be 'Follow-up meeting to discuss the project.'.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt is very specific: it names the new event title, date, start time, duration (1 hour), the exact description, and explicitly instructs to use the same participants as the identified existing event (title and date provided). Those details map directly to the required operations (find the existing event to get participants, then create a new event with the given fields). The only minor unspecified detail is time zone/format, but this is typically handled by the environment and doesn't prevent unambiguous execution. Overall, the task and constraints are explicit.

variations.L1: Set up a follow-up meeting for the project with the same people who attended the introductory meeting. Schedule it sometime soon after the introductory meeting—you can pick the exact day and time. Keep the purpose as a follow-up to discuss the project.
variations.L2: Create a 'Follow-up meeting' on 2024-05-16 for the same participants as the 'Introductory meeting' on 2024-05-15. Make it roughly one hour long and use the description 'Follow-up meeting to discuss the project.' You can choose a morning start time.
variations.L3: Please create a 1-hour event titled 'Follow-up meeting' on 2024-05-16 at 10:00 with the same participants as the 'Introductory meeting' on 2024-05-15. Use the description 'Follow-up meeting to discuss the project.' Find and copy the participant list from the introductory event yourself.
variations.L4: Please create a new 1-hour long event 'Follow-up meeting' on 2024-05-16 at 10:00 with the same participants as the 'Introductory meeting' on 2024-05-15. The description should be 'Follow-up meeting to discuss the project.' Add all participants exactly as listed in the introductory meeting.
variations.L5: First call search_calendar_events(query='Introductory meeting', date='2024-05-15') to retrieve that event's participants. Then call create_calendar_event(title='Follow-up meeting', description='Follow-up meeting to discuss the project.', start_time='2024-05-16 10:00', end_time='2024-05-16 11:00', participants=<participants_from_search>).
